subject: Re: M may not give expected results
body:
Thanks @Alister for the reply! I am working with the EMPIAR 10499 which 
lead to 3.5A in the WARP paper, so I probably did something wrong when 
importing the data (I also have the wrong map handedness - I guess I need 
to fix the tilt axis this before moving to M). 

@Hamid - In 
"https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/v8LxUD0_xUY/m/_2IYZYZpCwAJ", Alister 
mentions "If you run a refinement in M and it fails to improve things then 
various parameters are now likely overfited -> you should revert both your 
M project and the tilt series metadata files in your warp project to their 
state prior to refinement in M." Though I am not sure in practice how to 
revert the M or where the tilt series metadata files would be located


On Wednesday, August 30, 2023 at 4:47:55 PM UTC-4 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Nicolas,
>
> When you see something like this there are a few possibilities
>
> 1. data not strong enough for refinement of the more complex models used 
> in M
> 2. Something wrong with the project/import means the refinement is looking 
> for particles in the wrong place
>
> For 1. I often see people trying to refine time resolved poses and all 
> parameters with high resolution grids straight away - I tend to start with 
> a simpler model and add more when refinement stops improving. Everything is 
> prone to overfitting with weak data.
>
> For 2. I would do some sanity checks, export subtomos from M and average 
> them the same way you would in warp to check your species has imported 
> successfully 
>
> Cheers!
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On 30 Aug 2023, at 19:25, Nicolas Coudray <coud...@nyu.edu> wrote:
>
> ﻿
>
> Hi -
>
>  I have been trying to run M (1.1.0 beta)  on a windows station with 4 RTX 
> 3090 GPUs (cuda 12.2), but the sub-tomograms generated lead to a refinement 
> with worse resolution than before going through M. 
>  Looking more into the star file generated, I noticed the X,Y,Z 
> coordinates of the sub-tomograms were changing dramatically. I would expect 
> the coordinate in the output file to be equal to something like
> "(_rlnCoordinateX - _rlnOriginXAngst x PixelSize) x scaling_factor"  
>
> with scaling factor the difference between the pixelsize of the star file 
> given to M and the one generated after refinement in M. 
>
>  To try to debug, I imported a star file, and then immediately exported 
> exported sub-tomograms without refinement at the same pixel size as the 
> input one. I noticed the coordinates of the first sub-tomogram was as 
> expected, but all the others I checked had X,Y,Z, coodinates which were 
> significantly different from those in the input file. 
>
>  Has anyone any idea? I've seen other threads mentioning various issues 
> with RTX 3090, so I was wondering if it could be some configuration / 
> compatibility issue? Shall we downgrade to an older version of cuda (if so, 
> which one would you advise)?
>
> Thanks,
> Nicolas
>
> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
> "Warp" group.
> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an 
> email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.

> .
>
>
